events: May PCE inflation released today 8:30am ET — headline 4.1% YoY (3-year high), core 3.4% YoY, both in line with forecasts
Next FOMC meeting: July 28-29 — nine of eighteen members already penciling in at least one rate hike in 2026
Q2 earnings season begins in earnest mid-July (big banks ~July 13-17, Big Tech ~July 27-31)
rates: steady but hike likely — Fed held at 3.50-3.75% on June 17; dot plot median implies one 25bp hike by year-end 2026; money markets pricing near-certain hike by October
inflation: hot — headline PCE 4.1% YoY (highest since April 2023), core PCE 3.4% YoY; driven by energy prices tied to Iran conflict
valuation: elevated — trailing P/E ~25-32 depending on source, Shiller CAPE ~41 (well above long-run average of ~32)
futures_this_morning: S&P 500 futures +0.78%, Nasdaq 100 futures +2.15%, driven by Micron blowout earnings (+17%)
note: Futures are green thanks to Micron AI/chip euphoria, but that is a single-stock event. The macro backdrop is genuinely cautious: inflation printed at a 3-year high today, the Fed dot plot already leans hawkish with a hike likely before year-end, and the Shiller CAPE at 41 puts the broad market in expensive territory. SPY is 6.5% above its 200-day average. None of our candidates offer a clean margin of safety: MSFT is the closest at +5.4% MoS but our config demands a larger buffer under a cautious posture. Posture remains cautious.
